items.title: Xi meets Taiwan opposition chief, expresses ‘full confidence’ in Chinese, Taiwan unity
items.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping reiterated his opposition to Taiwanese independence during a meeting with Cheng Li-wun, leader of Taiwan's pro-Beijing opposition Kuomintang (KMT) party. Her official trip to Beijing, the first such visit in a decade, sparked controversy in the self-ruled island that China claims as its territory.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts hurtle home from Moon toward Pacific splashdown
items.summary: The Artemis II team headed for a Friday re-entry to Earth after a historic lunar mission, the first crewed mission to the Moon in over 50 years. The most crucial moments of the 10-day mission awaits them as they prepare for a splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of California.

items.title: Middle East live: Vance heads to Pakistan for Iran talks as Israeli assault on Lebanon tests truce
items.summary: US Vice President JD Vance sets off Friday for Pakistan to lead mediated talks with Iran in a bid to find a resolution to the war as massive Israeli strikes on Lebanon this week tested a fragile US-Iran ceasefire. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu authorised direct negotiations with Lebanon that are expected in Washington next week. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Netanyahu greenlights Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington amid ceasefire tensions
items.summary: Israel and Lebanon are set to hold talks in Washington next week as tensions escalate over continued fighting in Lebanon and concerns grow that the fragile US-Iran ceasefire could collapse. The meeting comes amid heavy strikes, rising casualties and renewed disputes over the scope of the truce and regional security arrangements.

items.title: Hip-hop pioneer Afrika Bambaataa dies at 68
items.summary: Afrika Bambaataa, one of hip-hop’s founding pioneers, died on Thursday at the age of 68 from prostate cancer, according to his lawyer. Known for shaping early hip-hop culture through DJing, electro-funk and the Zulu Nation collective, his legacy is both celebrated and contested amid longstanding abuse allegations.

items.title: NASA's Artemis II: conspiracy theories take off about ‘staged’ green screen
items.summary: NASA’s Artemis II is set to return to Earth on Friday, after its astronauts travelled further from Earth than any humans before. But the historic lunar flyby also inflamed conspiracy theories online about a “staged” mission, and triggered a wave of fake viral AI videos.

items.title: Nigeria begins trial of 500 terrorism suspects
items.summary: In tonight's edition, amid a surge of violence across Nigeria, Abuja begins mass trials of 500 suspected terrorists in 13 courtrooms under heavy security. Also, Benin votes this Sunday as Patrice Talon steps down after a decade in power. And Algerian boxing star Imane Khelif returns to the ring for her first pro fight since Olympic gold in Paris.

items.title: High fuel prices spur interest in EVs
items.summary: The war in Iran has sent fuel costs soaring, and consumers scrambling for savings. At the New York Auto Show, many drivers are warming up to the idea of buying an electric vehicle, because of high petrol prices, as our correspondent Jessica Le Masurier reports. Also in the show  - the relief rally loses steam as the fragile Iran ceasefire enters its second day, and the OECD decries a 'historic decline' in international development aid, driven by the US.

items.title: Melania Trump delivers statement denying Epstein ties, knowledge of his crimes
items.summary: US first lady Melania Trump gave an extraordinary statement from the White House on Thursday denying any ties to late financier and convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Trump said the "lies" linking her to Epstein were "mean-spirited attempts" to defame her reputation.

items.title: Putin announces Orthodox Easter ceasefire in Ukraine after Kyiv proposes same
items.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Thursday announced a ceasefire in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter weekend.

items.title: Valentin Vacherot continue son parcours de rêve à Monte-Carlo
items.summary: Le Monégasque Valentin Vacherot n’est plus qu’à trois victoires d’un rêve fou. Le n°23 mondial s’est qualifié pour les quarts de finale de son tournoi national après sa victoire, dans la douleur, contre Hubert Hurkacz. Il est le premier représentant de la principauté à atteindre les quarts de finale à Monte-Carlo. Un set de perdu, mais une qualification malgré tout pour les deux meilleurs joueurs du monde. Carlos Alcaraz et Jannik Sinner seront bien de la partie en quarts de finale à Monaco.

translation.title: Valentin Vacherot continues his dream journey in Monte-Carlo
translation.summary: Monegasque Valentin Vacherot is more than three victories from a crazy dream. World No.23 qualified for the quarter finals of his national tournament after his victory, in pain, against Hubert Hurkacz. He was the first representative of the principality to reach the quarterfinals in Monte Carlo. A set of lost, but a qualification despite everything for the two best players in the world. Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner will be in the quarterfinals in Monaco.

items.title: Détroit d'Ormuz : l'Iran impose sa loi
items.summary: Depuis le cessez-le-feu conclu entre l'Iran et les Etats-Unis, seuls quelques navires ont osé franchir le détroit d'Ormuz. Un pétrolier battant pavillon gabonais est devenu le premier tanker non-iranien à le faire. Pour en garder le contrôle, Téhéran affirme que des mines se trouvent au centre de la zone. translation.title: Ormuz Strait: Iran imposes its law
translation.summary: Since the ceasefire between Iran and the United States, only a few ships have dared to cross the Strait of Ormuz. A Gabonese tanker became the first non-Iranian tanker to do so. To maintain control, Tehran claims that mines are in the centre of the area. The Islamic Republic also defends the idea of a permanent toll. An option that tries Donald Trump, but divides the Gulf countries. The European Union, starting with France, the European

items.title: Trêve au Moyen-Orient : qui peut agir ?
items.summary: Alors que des pourparlers entre les États-Unis, Israël et l'Iran doivent débuter vendredi à Islamabad, au Pakistan. Le Premier ministre israélien Benjamin Netanyahu a annoncé jeudi avoir ordonné à son cabinet d'engager des "négociations directes" avec le Liban. De son côté, Donald Trump assure que l'armée américaine restera déployée à proximité de l'Iran jusqu'à l'application complète d'un "réel accord".

translation.title: Truce in the Middle East: who can act?
translation.summary: As talks between the United States, Israel and Iran begin on Friday in Islamabad, Pakistan. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced on Thursday that he had ordered his cabinet to engage in "direct negotiations" with Lebanon. Donald Trump, for his part, assures that the US army will remain deployed near Iran until a "real deal" is fully implemented.

items.title: Cessez-le-feu en Iran : le divorce consommé entre Trump et l'Europe ?
items.summary: Après cinq semaines de frappes, le cessez-le-feu brandi par Donald Trump résonne comme une victoire en trompe-l’œil. Fidèle à sa rhétorique, le président américain impose son propre récit pour masquer une réalité plus amère : un accord fragile où l'Iran garde la main sur le détroit d'Ormuz.

translation.title: Stop the fire in Iran: the consummated divorce between Trump and Europe?
translation.summary: After five weeks of strikes, Donald Trump's cease-fire resonates like a trompe-l'oeil victory. True to his rhetoric, the U.S. president imposed his own narrative to mask a bitterer reality: a fragile agreement in which Iran kept its hand on the Strait of Ormuz.

items.title: Mission Artémis II : les images prouvent-elles une mise en scène de la NASA ?
items.summary: La mission Artémis II vient de marquer l'histoire : quatre astronautes ont survolé la face cachée de la Lune, s'aventurant plus loin de la Terre que n'importe quel être humain avant eux. Un exploit scientifique qui, comme en 1969, n’a pas tardé à exciter l'imagination des complotistes sur les réseaux sociaux. Entre faux fonds verts et images générées par l'intelligence artificielle, Info/Intox démêle le vrai du faux.

translation.title: Mission Artemis II: Do the images prove a NASA staging?
translation.summary: The Artemis II mission has just marked history: four astronauts flew over the hidden face of the Moon, adventured further from the Earth than any human being before them. A scientific feat which, as in 1969, soon aroused the imagination of the plotters on social networks. Between false green backgrounds and images generated by artificial intelligence, Info/Intox unravels the true of the fake.

items.title: Présidentielle au Bénin : Patrice Talon, de "golden-boy" à chef d'État omnipotent
items.summary: Élu pour la première fois en 2016 à la tête du Bénin, Patrice Talon quittera la présidence après l'élection de dimanche. Homme d'affaires à succès converti à la politique, l'ancien roi du coton, a connu un parcours mouvementé avant d'accéder à la fonction suprême. Il laisse derrière lui un pays transformé d'un point de vue économique mais divisé par son tournant autoritaire.

translation.title: President in Benin: Patrice Talon, from "golden-boy" to omnipotent head of state
translation.summary: First elected in 2016 as head of Benin, Patrice Talon will leave the presidency after Sunday's election. A successful businessman converted to politics, the former king of cotton, went through a turbulent journey before entering the supreme office. He left behind a country transformed from an economic point of view but divided by its authoritarian turn.

items.title: Moyen-Orient en direct : J.D. Vance attendu au Pakistan pour des pourparlers sur l'Iran
items.summary: Le vice-président américain JD Vance doit mener la délégation des États-Unis pour des discussions samedi sur l'Iran, aux côtés de l'émissaire spécial Steve Witkoff et Jared Kushner, a annoncé la Maison Blanche. La participation des Iraniens reste incertaine après des frappes israéliennes qui ont fait mercredi plus de 300 morts au Liban. Malgré des positions divergentes, Donald Trump s'est dit "très optimiste" sur la possibilité de conclure un accord de paix avec Téhéran. Suivez notre direct.

translation.title: Middle East live: J.D. Vance expected in Pakistan for talks on Iran
translation.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance is to lead the US delegation for discussions on Iran on Saturday, alongside Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, announced the White House. The participation of Iranians remains uncertain following Israeli strikes, which on Wednesday killed more than 300 people in Lebanon. Despite divergent positions, Donald Trump said he was "very optimistic" about the possibility of concluding a peace agreement with Tehran. Follow our direct.

items.title: Emmanuel Macron rencontre pour la première fois le pape Léon XIV, le Liban au cœur de leurs échanges
items.summary: Emmanuel Macron rencontre vendredi Léon XIV au Vatican, près d'un an après l'élection du pape américain qui, comme lui, s'est démarqué de la rhétorique guerrière de Donald Trump. Cette première rencontre entre les deux hommes devrait être dominée par le conflit au Moyen-Orient et une préoccupation partagée pour le Liban.

translation.title: Emmanuel Macron meets for the first time Pope Leo XIV, Lebanon at the heart of their exchanges
translation.summary: Emmanuel Macron met on Friday Leo XIV in the Vatican, almost a year after the election of the American pope who, like him, stood out from Donald Trump's war rhetoric. This first meeting between the two men should be dominated by the conflict in the Middle East and a shared concern for Lebanon.

items.title: Guerre au Moyen-Orient : Washington va accueillir des pourparlers entre Israël et le Liban
items.summary: Un responsable américain a affirmé jeudi que Washington accueillerait la semaine prochaine une réunion "pour discuter des négociations de cessez-le-feu avec Israël et le Liban". En attendant, les frappes israéliennes contre le Hezbollah continuent. Ce vendredi, c'est le Pakistan qui accueille des pourparlers diplomatiques, cette fois entre l'Iran et les États-Unis.

translation.title: Middle East War: Washington to host talks between Israel and Lebanon
translation.summary: A US official said on Thursday that Washington would host a meeting next week "to discuss ceasefire negotiations with Israel and Lebanon". Meanwhile, Israeli strikes against Hezbollah continue. This Friday, Pakistan is hosting diplomatic talks, this time between Iran and the United States.

items.title: Mission Artémis II : l'heure du retour sur Terre a sonné pour les quatre astronautes
items.summary: Après avoir fait le tour de la Lune, les quatre astronautes de la mission Artémis II seront de retour sur Terre vendredi soir. La capsule Orion transportant les Américains Christina Koch, Victor Glover et Reid Wiseman et le Canadien Jeremy Hansen doit se poser au large de San Diego en fin de journée.

translation.title: Mission Artemis II: time for the four astronauts to return to Earth
translation.summary: After visiting the Moon, the four astronauts of the Artemis II mission will return to Earth on Friday night. The Orion capsule carrying Americans Christina Koch, Victor Glover and Reid Wiseman and Canadian Jeremy Hansen is scheduled to land off San Diego at the end of the day.

The main elements of the census include agricultural production conditions, food and food production, new quality of agricultural productivity, basics of rural development, living conditions of rural residents, etc. The standard time of the census is 2400 hours on 31 December 2026, and the data for the period is annual 2026. items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee on Energy Conservation Law Enforcement Inspection Unit that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection took the form of a combination of on-site inspections and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, typical research, thematic studies, spot-checking and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with the law; other issues in the implementation of the law; and suggestions and recommendations for changes to the energy-saving law.

items.title: Russia adds 2 suspects in killing of Chechen woman to federal wanted list
items.summary: Russia’s Interior Ministry has added Karina Iminova and Said-Khamzat Baisarov to its federal wanted database, the SK SOS Crisis Group, a human rights organization, reported. Both are suspected of involvement in the killing of Aishat Baimuradova, a woman from Chechnya.

items.title: Russia declares federal emergency in 2 regions amid severe flooding
items.summary: Russia’s Emergency Situations Minister Alexander Kurenkov declared a federal state of emergency in Dagestan and Chechnya on April 9 following severe flooding in both regions.

items.title: Putin orders Easter ceasefire, calls on Ukraine to follow
items.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin declared an “Easter ceasefire” from 4:00 p.m. on April 11 through the end of the day on April 12, the Kremlin announced on April 9.

items.title: Russia’s Justice Ministry identifies 196 active members of Memorial movement, now banned as ‘extremist’
items.summary: Russia’s Justice Ministry announced that at least 196 people are actively involved in the International Memorial movement, which the Supreme Court has designated an extremist organization.

Allegations of unaccounted cash surfaced after a fire at his residence, prompting a Supreme Court inquiry and subsequent parliamentary action. The judge had previously questioned the constitutionality of the inquiry process.

items.title: ‘Did nothing wrong’: Threat backlash forces Shreyas' sister to delete viral video

Despite clarifying her intent was supportive and not hateful, she deleted the clip to prevent negativity and urged for kindness.

items.title: Mukul Choudhary's untold story: Jail, loans & sacrifice - A father’s 3.5 cr gamble

items.title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ earns Rs 1,665 cr in 3 weeks, misses ‘Dhurandhar’ record
